questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.explanation: Selling templates is mentioned as a lesson topic, not the main purpose.

options.text: To turn curiosity into capability by teaching Notion, building workflows, and offering badges
options.explanation: The article states the goal is to turn curiosity into capability, learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and earn badges.

options.text: To provide a marketplace for Notion apps
options.explanation: The marketplace is referenced in a lesson, not as the Academy’s overarching goal.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center
options.explanation: The Help Center is still listed as a separate resource for quick answers.

questions.question: Which credential can you earn by completing the certification track in Notion Academy?

options.explanation: The article explicitly mentions the "Notion Certified Admin" certification.
While badges exist, the specific certification is the Admin credential.
Marketplace vendor status is not a certification offered by the Academy.
Developer tokens are technical assets, not a learning credential.

questions.question: What type of learning path is described as "New to Notion? Gain the basic skills to create, collaborate, and organize your work efficiently"?

options.explanation: The Advanced Path focuses on automation and complex capabilities.
The description matches the Essentials Path, aimed at beginners with 3 lessons.
Workflows Path is for intermediate learners creating connected systems.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a multi‑lesson path.

questions.question: Which path specifically mentions the use of AI to create connected systems?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is for beginners and does not reference AI.
The Workflows Path description includes "Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI."
Advanced Path focuses on automation and architecture but does not explicitly mention AI.
That path is about creating and selling templates, not AI‑driven systems.

questions.question: How long is the "Custom Agents" lesson?

options.explanation: 15 minutes is the length of several other lessons, but not Custom Agents.
The article lists the Custom Agents lesson as 30 min.
20 minutes corresponds to the "Building basics" lesson.
40 minutes is the duration of the "Templates & marketplace" lesson.

questions.question: Which lesson would you take to learn how to create, list, market, and sell Notion templates?

options.explanation: Workspace design focuses on layout best practices, not templates.
The lesson title and description directly address creating, listing, marketing, and selling templates.
Custom Agents deals with automation, not template commerce.
That lesson covers teamwork features, not template creation.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of becoming a Notion Certified Admin?

options.text: Access to free premium Notion templates
options.explanation: Certification does not grant template access; it validates admin skills.

options.text: Recognition for running Notion at scale, implementing security best practices, and managing workspaces across any plan type
options.explanation: The article states certification lets you run Notion at scale, apply security best practices, and be recognized as an admin for any plan.

options.text: Automatic enrollment in all advanced learning paths
options.explanation: Certification is a credential; it does not automatically enroll you in paths.

options.text: Lifetime free access to the Notion Help Center
options.explanation: The Help Center is publicly available; certification does not affect access.

questions.question: How does a "Badged Path" differ from a single lesson in Notion Academy?

options.text: A Badged Path consists of multiple lessons and awards a badge upon completion, whereas a single lesson is a standalone module with no badge attached
options.explanation: Badged Paths are structured collections (e.g., Essentials, Workflows, Advanced) that grant a badge after finishing all contained lessons. Individual lessons are independent and do not provide a badge on their own.

options.text: Badged Paths are only available to premium Notion users, while single lessons are free for all users
options.explanation: The article does not mention any paywall distinction between paths and lessons.

options.text: Badged Paths focus exclusively on technical automation, whereas single lessons cover only soft‑skill topics
options.explanation: Both paths and lessons span a range of topics, from basics to automation.

options.text: Badged Paths are delivered via live webinars, while single lessons are pre‑recorded videos
options.explanation: The delivery format (live or recorded) is not tied to the Badged Path designation.

questions.question: Which of the following resources is NOT listed as a way to continue learning after completing a Notion Academy lesson?

options.explanation: The Help Center is explicitly mentioned as a resource.
Live webinars are listed as a learning option.
Joining the community is encouraged in the article.
The article does not mention a one‑on‑one mentorship program.

questions.question: What level of difficulty is the "Workspace design" lesson categorized as?

options.explanation: Beginner lessons are typically 10‑20 minutes; Workspace design is marked as Advanced.
Intermediate lessons are labeled as such; Workspace design is not.
The article lists Workspace design as an Advanced lesson lasting 10 minutes.
The article uses "Advanced" rather than "Expert" for this lesson.
